text.title: Usability Testing — 7-Step Process
text.summary: Structured procedure for planning, conducting, and reporting moderated or unmoderated usability sessions.

section.title: Step 1: Define Goals

rule.statement: State test goals as answerable questions, not topics. Each question must be tied to a measurable metric.
rule.rationale: Vague goals ("understand navigation") produce unactionable findings. Specific questions ("Can users complete checkout in under 3 minutes?") produce pass/fail evidence.

section.p: Examples: Can users complete checkout in under 3 minutes? Do users understand the navigation structure? What blocks users from signing up?

rule.statement: Document objectives, participant profile, tasks, metrics, and environment before recruiting.
rule.rationale: Recruiting before task design means participants may not match the tasks. Plan first, recruit second.

rule.statement: Tasks must use realistic scenarios with clear goals and zero hints about the solution path.
rule.rationale: Hints in task wording ("click the Account button") inflate task success rates and produce false confidence in design quality.

example.note: Scenario: You need to change your delivery address for an upcoming order. Task: Find your recent order and update the shipping address. Success: User locates order and changes address without prompting.
Task: "Click the account button and select orders." Gives away the solution path; measures ability to follow instructions, not ability to navigate.

rule.statement: Recruit 5-8 participants per distinct user segment; match the target user profile including experience level mix.
rule.rationale: Fewer than 5 participants per segment misses recurring patterns. Segments with different mental models must be tested separately.

section.title: Step 5: Conduct Sessions

rule.statement: Facilitate without helping: do not answer questions, guide navigation, or react to errors during tasks.
rule.rationale: Any facilitator intervention invalidates the task result — it measures the facilitator's skill, not the product's usability.

section.p: Session structure: Introduction 5 min, Pre-test questions 5 min, Tasks 30-45 min, Post-task questions 10 min, Debrief 5 min.

rule.statement: Assign severity ratings (Critical/High/Medium/Low) to every finding before reporting.
rule.rationale: Unrated findings produce undifferentiated lists that teams cannot prioritize. Severity ratings force triage.

list.item: Critical — users cannot complete the task. Fix immediately.
High — significant difficulty completing. Fix before launch.
Medium — some struggle. Fix soon after launch.
Low — minor friction. Fix when possible.

rule.statement: Each finding must include: severity, affected task, frequency (N of Y participants), problem description, evidence, and a concrete recommendation.
rule.rationale: Findings without recommendations leave design decisions to whoever reads the report. Researchers must propose the fix.
